worlds.description: Prioritizes audited senior/applied evidence, keeps generic ML borderline, hard-excludes strongest honeypot proxies.
worlds.name: conservative

worlds.description: Gives the plain-language and explicit senior cohorts the strongest Tier-5 prior, matching the JD's senior-system-builder anchor.
worlds.name: senior_heavy

worlds.description: Raises applied-ML candidates when their career templates show ranking/evaluation depth, while keeping senior cohorts high.
worlds.name: applied_ml_friendly

worlds.description: Stress-tests whether generic-ML or data/backend tail exceptions can enter the shortlist when skill overlays are useful.
worlds.name: generic_tail_friendly

worlds.description: Uses the same static structure as the conservative world but gives bounded behavior and availability the largest allowed influence.
worlds.name: behavior_medium

worlds.description: Treats detected honeypot proxies as very strong penalties rather than absolute truth, to measure mutation-policy sensitivity.
worlds.name: mutation_uncertain
